key_insight: Thermal spray methods can deposit thick, functional polymer coatings on diverse substrates while balancing adhesion, substrate protection, and tailored surface properties.
engineering_relevance: The review is relevant for designing durable, multifunctional protective coatings for corrosion, wear, hydrophobicity, and biocompatible applications in surface engineering.
method: Compares thermal spray routes for polymers: flame spraying, HVOF, plasma spraying, and cold spraying.
Reviews coating formation, microstructure, mechanical behavior, and functional performance.
Summarizes suitability of common polymers and polymer-based composites such as PE, PA, PI, PU, PEEK, PET, and fluoropolymers.
evidence_signal: The abstract indicates a literature review synthesis rather than new experimental data, evaluating reported process–structure–property relationships and application outcomes across multiple spray techniques.
watch_item: Polymer thermal spraying can be sensitive to thermal degradation, adhesion control, and process–material compatibility, so parameters must be carefully tuned for each polymer/substrate pair.
paper_title: A Review - Thermal spraying on Polymers
